evidence_quotes.quote: Ive purchased many digital games and itemssome using systems like Microsoft Points.
Unfortunately, a significant amount of this content is no longer accessible due to online servers being shut down or platforms not supporting older titles.
If I buy a game or item, I should be able to download and use it across any of my personal deviceslike PCs or consoleswithout being forced to stay online or go through restrictive DRM checks.
Its important that digital products remain usable even without an internet connection.
Many older titles are impossible to play today due to platform incompatibilities and discontinued services.

I strongly support legislation that ensures digital purchases remain accessible, transferable, and usable without unnecessary online restrictions, and that empowers consumers with the tools to preserve and self-host content theyve paid for.

paragraphs.text: As a lifelong gamer and frequent user of digital platforms, Id like to share my experience and highlight several issues I hope the Digital Fairness Act will address: Loss of Access to Purchased Content - Over the years, Ive purchased many digital games and itemssome using systems like Microsoft Points. Unfortunately, a significant amount of this content is no longer accessible due to online servers being shut down or platforms not supporting older titles. This leads to the loss of content I paid for and invested time in, which is deeply frustrating. Digital Ownership and Cross-Device Freedom - Consumers should have real ownership of digital goods. If I buy a game or item, I should be able to download and use it across any of my personal deviceslike PCs or consoleswithout being forced to stay online or go through restrictive DRM checks. Digital goods should work as freely as physical ones. Offline Access and Self-Hosting Options - Its important that digital products remain usable even without an internet connection. In cases where online features are critical (such as multiplayer servers), users should be given the legal right and technical tools to self-host or emulate those services, especially if the original provider shuts them down. This would help preserve the functionality and value of our purchases. Preservation and Legacy Support - Many older titles are impossible to play today due to platform incompatibilities and discontinued services. Consumers need stronger guarantees that their digital libraries wont be lost over time due to external decisions beyond their control. I strongly support legislation that ensures digital purchases remain accessible, transferable, and usable without unnecessary online restrictions, and that empowers consumers with the tools to preserve and self-host content theyve paid for.
